adrs.title: Always the best solution, never the easiest
adrs.tldr: Best, senior-level, production-grade — no shortcuts or unfinished leftovers; fix, never silently remove a feature (removal needs the owner's consent).

adrs.title: Repository is the single source of truth; memory is repo-resident
adrs.tldr: All decisions/rules/memory live in the repo; agent memory sits in .claude/memory and is symlinked into the user memory dir, so there is one truth and no drift.

adrs.title: Verify first, never guess — every claim must be provable
adrs.tldr: Every claim must be verified and provable (source/test/measurement); no guessing; unverified items are marked 'open', never asserted as fact.

adrs.title: Reusability policy — no structural duplication, shared single sources
adrs.tldr: Reuse over copy-paste; one source for cross-cutting concerns (ts-rs types, HUD theme, command wrappers) — no duplicated structure.

adrs.title: Context budget — TLDR index + selective on-demand loading + compact-survival
adrs.tldr: Boot loads only CLAUDE.md + TLDR indexes + core docs; full ADRs/rules load on demand via tldr/triggers/applies-to; hooks force a reload after compact.

adrs.title: Generated indexes + staleness gate
adrs.tldr: One generator builds all indexes from doc front-matter; a content-hash scanner fails CI on drift.

adrs.title: Unified quality pipeline (check:all), pre-commit and CI
adrs.tldr: One check:all gate (TS + Rust + governance) runs locally via pre-commit + pre-push; CI is last-line backstop and builds only final releases, never dev builds.

adrs.title: Dependency policy — latest stable everything, verified, lockfiles committed
adrs.tldr: Latest stable of every dependency (verified vs crates.io/npm), lockfiles committed — current versions minimise known CVEs and avoid an update cycle.

adrs.title: Testing strategy — test-first (TDD), unit tests from the first module, contracts pinned both sides
adrs.tldr: Test-first (TDD): the failing test comes before the code; every module ships unit tests; IPC contracts pinned both sides; coverage gated in check:all.

adrs.title: Security by design — hardened Tauri, validated IPC, no secret leakage
adrs.tldr: Credentials in the OS keyring and never in the frontend; least-privilege capabilities, strict CSP, validated IPC inputs, app-data-only writes, HTTPS-only.

adrs.title: Agent delegation — model selection and subagent governance inheritance
adrs.tldr: Pick the right model per task (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku), brief each subagent with a task-fit senior persona, and make it inherit + never bypass project governance.

adrs.title: Versioning (SSOT + SemVer) and dev/release build channels
adrs.tldr: package.json is the version SSOT (synced into Cargo.toml + Cargo.lock, gated in check:all); every change bumps SemVer; releasing is a separate, instructed act.

adrs.title: Portable governance core — versioned, hash-pinned, updatable across template forks
adrs.tldr: SUPERSEDED by ADR-033: two layers became N ordered layers. The hash pin, drift gate and self-updating governance:update are retained there.

adrs.title: Config layering — pinned core config, project overlays, explicit opt-out
adrs.tldr: Core config stays pinned; project-specific config is project-owned; eslint/knip take a project overlay; governance/opt-out.json opts a core path out of the pin.

adrs.title: Governance layers — an agnostic core, an application layer, and a publishing cascade
adrs.tldr: Governance is N ordered layers (core → app → project); a repo consumes upstream layers, may own one, and republishes the union to its own forks.
